metadata.title: System Architecture — Multi-Agent Mining Accident Investigation System
metadata.thesis_contribution: Extends Markarian's 6-subsystem architecture by replacing the classical propositional logic reasoning layer (subsystems 4-6) with a multi-agent LLM system using Dung's argumentation semantics for conflict resolution

end_goal.description: The system takes already-completed investigations where expert reports exist, and applies formal argumentation to resolve conflicts between experts and produce explainable conclusions. The input is pre-structured expert findings.
test_cases: Kostenko mine explosion (Kazakhstan, 2023) — primary test case
Upper Big Branch mine explosion (USA, 2010) — secondary test case for generalizability
end_goal.knowledge_base_strategy: Anna's Rostechnadzor materials define the structure; MSHA public data provides the volume. English-language primary, Russian-language as structural benchmark.

category_A_incident_evidence.content: Kostenko: 17 arguments from 3 expert reports. UBB: to be extracted from MSHA/McAteer/WV state reports.

category_B_safety_regulations.content: 7 Rostechnadzor regulation summaries. US 30 CFR framework to be added for UBB.
category_B_safety_regulations.format: Structured JSON with topic, requirement, applicable standard

category_C_precedent_cases.content: 7 Rostechnadzor cases (structural template). Target 15-25 total using MSHA major disaster reports.
category_C_precedent_cases.format: Structured JSON with cause categories, relevance scores

category_D_domain_knowledge.content: LLM training data serves as domain knowledge. Acknowledged as limitation in thesis.
category_D_domain_knowledge.rationale: Adding a full mining engineering textbook to KB would be huge effort with marginal benefit for prototype

category_E_taxonomies.content: 10 technical + 7 organizational cause categories from Rostechnadzor two-tier classification
category_E_taxonomies.format: Structured JSON with id, label, description

v1_decomposition.markarian_original: TF-IDF + SBERT embeddings converting raw text into binary propositions (frames)

mode_1_primary.name: Structured JSON input
mode_1_primary.description: Investigator has already extracted arguments into JSON format. System loads and validates directly.
mode_1_primary.when_used: Both test cases (Kostenko, UBB) for thesis evaluation

mode_2_demonstration.name: LLM-assisted extraction
mode_2_demonstration.description: System accepts raw text passage from investigation report, LLM extracts structured arguments.
mode_2_demonstration.when_used: Proves the pipeline can work end-to-end. Evaluated against manual extraction as ground truth.

output_schema.schema_justification: Derived from Toulmin's argument model (L-19): claim, data (evidence), warrant

our_design.key_property: Both modes produce identical output schema — everything downstream is mode-agnostic

literature_justification: L-01 (Markarian et al. 2025): establishes NLP-based evidence extraction approach
L-19 (Toulmin 1958): argument structure theory justifying the 8-field schema

v2_identification.markarian_original: Multi-label logistic regression classifying event type from features

our_design.method: Rule-based mapping from cause_categories to accident types
our_design.input: All arguments from v1 with their cause_categories tags
our_design.process: Aggregate cause categories across all arguments, match against accident type definitions in taxonomy

output.secondary_types: Additional types if agents disagree (e.g., coal_dust_explosion)
output.cause_profile: Frequency count of cause categories across all arguments

our_design.simplification_rationale: Classification is not our thesis contribution. Rule-based sufficient for prototype. Thesis notes that production system would use trained ML classifiers per Markarian et al. 2025.

literature_justification: L-01 (Markarian et al. 2025): multi-label classification approach
L-16 (Analysis of coal mine accidents 2024): cause classification methodology

v3_precedent_matching.markarian_original: CBR with correspondence matrix, threshold δ ≥ 0.8

our_design.method: Two-step CBR: type filter + Jaccard cause category overlap
our_design.step_1: Filter precedents by compatible accident type
our_design.input: Accident type and cause profile from v2
our_design.output: Ranked list of precedent matches with overlap scores
our_design.data_source: Searches only the knowledge base (precedent store). Limited to loaded cases.
our_design.coverage_limitation: Acknowledged in thesis. Quality scales with KB coverage. Prototype uses 15-25 cases; production system would benefit from hundreds.
our_design.simplification_rationale: Jaccard overlap is simpler than Markarian's full correspondence matrix but serves same purpose — identifying most relevant past cases

literature_justification: L-02 (Markarian & Temkin 2024): CBR methodology and correspondence matrix
L-13 (Zhao 2024): ontology-based coal mine accident case retrieval
L-14 (CBR for gas explosion 2025): practical CBR in mining safety domain

v4_specialist_agents.markarian_original: Hypothesis formation via propositional logic: D: (x₁ & x₂ & ... & xₙ) ⟹ yₘ

our_design.approach: 4 LLM agents with specialized prompts and RAG access to knowledge base

agents.name: Technical causes analyst
agents.focus: Physical mechanisms: ignition source, gas accumulation, equipment involvement, explosion dynamics
agents.scope_overlap: Overlaps with agents 2 and 3 on ignition source and root cause

agents.name: Organizational and human factors analyst
agents.focus: Systemic failures: supervision gaps, procedure violations, training deficiencies, management decisions
agents.scope_overlap: Overlaps with agents 1 and 3 on root cause attribution

agents.name: Independent challenger
agents.focus: Alternative explanations, questions assumptions, identifies what evidence does NOT support
agents.analogous_to: DMT's skeptical position on ignition source
agents.scope_overlap: Deliberately overlaps with agents 1 and 2 to generate conflicts

agents.name: Regulatory compliance checker
agents.focus: Evidence vs applicable safety standards, identifies regulatory violations
agents.analogous_to: No direct Kostenko analogue — adds regulatory dimension
agents.scope_overlap: Complements rather than competes with agents 1-3

design_rationale.why_4_agents: Agents 1-3 have intentionally overlapping scope to generate genuine conflicts for v5. Agent 4 adds complementary regulatory analysis.
design_rationale.why_overlap_matters: Non-overlapping agents rarely produce contradictory claims. Overlapping scope on topics like ignition source and explosion mechanism naturally generates the attack relations v5 needs.
design_rationale.why_heterogeneous: A-HMAD (L-06) shows heterogeneous specialized agents outperform homogeneous ones. Each agent has a different analytical lens.

our_design.each_agent_outputs: One or more claims in the v1 output schema: {id, source, topic, claim, evidence, warrant, confidence, cause_categories}
our_design.combined_output: All agent claims merged into a single claim set for v5

literature_justification: L-04 (Du et al. 2023): multi-agent LLM debate improves reasoning
L-05 (Liang et al. 2024): distinct roles with moderate disagreement outperforms identical agents
L-06 (A-HMAD 2025): heterogeneous specialized agents outperform homogeneous
L-17 (Sun et al. 2025): LLMs applied to coal mine safety with domain KB

v5_argumentation_layer.markarian_original: Propositional calculus truth tables: D evaluated as tautology/contradiction/contingent, probability e = (1/s) × Σ eₖ

our_design.approach: Dung's abstract argumentation framework with both grounded and preferred semantics

step_1_conflict_detection.method: Hybrid: topic-based filter then LLM confirmation
step_1_conflict_detection.topic_filter: Identify claim pairs sharing the same topic field — these are potential conflicts
step_1_conflict_detection.llm_confirmation: Feed each candidate pair to LLM: 'Do these claims contradict? Is it rebutting or undercutting?'

attack_types.rebutting: Claims reach directly contradictory conclusions (mutual attack)
attack_types.undercutting: One claim undermines the evidence/warrant of another (directed attack)

step_1_conflict_detection.support_detection: Same-topic claims with compatible conclusions flagged as support (tracked separately)

step_2_af_construction.nodes: Each claim from v4 = one node with all schema attributes
step_2_af_construction.edges: Each detected attack = one directed edge with attack type label
step_2_af_construction.support: Stored as separate data structure (not edges in Dung's AF)

grounded.algorithm: Iterative fixpoint of characteristic function F(S): add all acceptable arguments, repeat until stable
grounded.properties: Unique extension, most skeptical, always exists
grounded.interpretation: Arguments the system is confident about — defended against all attacks

preferred.algorithm: Find all maximal admissible sets (brute-force for <25 arguments, labelling-based for larger sets)
preferred.interpretation: Each extension represents one defensible worldview

step_4_comparison.grounded_equals_preferred: Clear consensus — no genuine ambiguity in the evidence
step_4_comparison.grounded_subset_preferred: Genuine ambiguity exists — some arguments are defensible but contested
step_4_comparison.significance: The comparison itself is a finding: it tells the investigator whether the evidence is conclusive or genuinely ambiguous

output.accepted_arguments: In the grounded extension — confident conclusions
output.rejected_arguments: Not in any preferred extension — defeated hypotheses
output.ambiguous_arguments: In some preferred extensions but not all — genuinely unresolved
output.full_graph: Complete AF with all nodes, edges, and extension membership for visualization

literature_justification: L-08 (Dung 1995): THE foundational paper — defines AF, grounded, preferred, admissibility
L-09 (Panisson 2021): computational argumentation in MAS — shows tractability
L-10 (Carrera 2015 review): systematic review of argumentation techniques in MAS
L-12 (Vassiliades 2021): argumentation as explanation mechanism for XAI
v5_argumentation_layer.research_gap_this_fills: Existing LLM multi-agent debate (Du et al. 2023, Liang et al. 2024) uses informal consensus (majority voting). We replace this with mathematically grounded conflict resolution. The attack graph structure determines outcomes, not headcount.

v6_report_generation.markarian_original: Select hypothesis with highest probability: y*: e(y*) = max{eₘ}

our_design.approach: LLM-generated natural language report populated from v5 structured output
our_design.key_property: Full traceability — every conclusion traces back: accepted claim → evidence → agent → attack structure

output_formats.markdown_html: Rendered report for human reading and defense presentation

our_design.graph_visualization: NetworkX export → rendered with nodes colored by accepted/rejected/ambiguous

literature_justification: L-12 (Vassiliades 2021): argumentation graph as explanation artifact
L-03 (Markarian 2025): final report as decision support output (automated support mode, not replacement)

test_case_1.ground_truth: Manually extracted 17 arguments with known attack/support relations from 3 real expert reports
test_case_1.evaluation: Compare system's argumentation output against the known expert disagreements

test_case_2.ground_truth: To be extracted from MSHA, McAteer, and WV state investigation reports
test_case_2.evaluation: Same comparison — does the system identify the known conflicts between investigators?

mode_2_evaluation.description: Feed raw report text through LLM extraction (v1 Mode 2), compare extracted arguments against manual extraction
mode_2_evaluation.metrics: Argument recall, claim accuracy, evidence completeness

semantics_evaluation.description: Compare grounded vs preferred extensions across both cases
semantics_evaluation.metrics: Number of accepted/rejected/ambiguous arguments, whether grounded = preferred
